summary: This PR removed the remaining clang errors and warnings in the Melee decompilation tree, primarily to make clang-based tooling and future CI checks viable. The PR body emphasizes include-path refactoring: relative #include statements were validated under both MWCC and clang, editor resolution was checked with clangd in Neovim and Visual Studio Code, Vim/Neovim support was maintained through tools/vimrc, and m2ctx was updated to use the corrected paths. No changed-file or diff details were present in the supplied slice, so exact symbol-level edits are unknown.

key_files.reason: Mentioned in the PR body as supporting Vim/Neovim include resolution without a language server.
The PR body states that m2ctx was updated to use the correct include paths, but the exact file path was not available in the slice.
Described as a normal include directory after the include-path refactor.
Described as an included shorthand path.

what_changed: Resolved the remaining clang errors and warnings reported by clang tooling.
Refactored relative #include usage so paths work under both MWCC and clang.
Updated m2ctx path handling to use the corrected include paths.
Established src/melee as a normal include directory.
Treated other libraries as system includes.
Added or preserved src/melee/ft/chara as a shorthand include path.
Verified include resolution in Neovim and Visual Studio Code using clangd.
Verified Vim/Neovim include resolution without a language server when sourcing tools/vimrc.
Prepared the project for a future clang warning/error check in CI, though CI integration was explicitly not implemented in this PR.
smart_moves: Validated include-path changes against both MWCC and clang, reducing the risk that editor/tooling cleanup would break the matching compiler workflow.
Checked editor behavior across clangd-based setups and Vim/Neovim without clangd, showing awareness that decomp contributors use varied development environments.
Updated m2ctx alongside source include paths so context generation would not drift from the new include layout.
Separated normal include directories from system include directories, which is useful for clang diagnostics and toolchain behavior.
Deferred CI integration until after the clang warning/error baseline was clean, avoiding a premature CI gate.
decomp_lessons: When cleaning headers or include paths in a decomp project, test against both the matching compiler, here MWCC, and modern analysis compilers such as clang.
Tooling compatibility should not require preserving awkward full system include paths if relative includes and include-directory configuration are made consistent.
m2ctx and editor/tooling configuration need to be updated together with source include refactors; otherwise generated context or clangd behavior may diverge from source reality.
A clean clang baseline is a useful prerequisite for adding CI checks, but the CI enforcement can be a separate follow-up once warnings are eliminated.
Include-directory policy should be explicit: the PR body documents src/melee as normal, other libraries as system includes, and src/melee/ft/chara as a shorthand.
naming_conventions: The PR reinforces using relative #include statements rather than full system include paths where possible.
src/melee is treated as a primary normal include root.
Non-melee libraries are treated as system include roots.
src/melee/ft/chara is available as a shorthand include path.
No symbol, function, struct, or typedef naming changes were visible in the provided slice.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The include refactor was tested with MWCC, indicating that clang compatibility work was constrained to preserve the matching compiler path.
No assembly-specific matching tricks, instruction-ordering changes, or function-level decomp tactics were visible in the provided slice.

The practical matching tactic here is infrastructure-oriented: keep context generation, compiler include paths, and editor include resolution synchronized so future matching work has reliable diagnostics.
